taskDescription: Arrange the components of the brachial plexus from proximal to distal in the correct order.
paragraphs: Roots: ventral rami of C5, C6, C7, C8, and T1 emerge between scalenus anterior and scalenus medius.
Trunks: C5+C6 form the upper trunk, C7 continues as the middle trunk, C8+T1 form the lower trunk (in the posterior triangle).
Divisions: each trunk divides into anterior and posterior divisions (behind the clavicle).
Cords: anterior divisions form lateral and medial cords; all posterior divisions form the posterior cord (in the axilla, named by relation to the axillary artery).
Terminal branches: musculocutaneous, median, ulnar, radial, and axillary nerves emerge from the cords to supply the upper limb.

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
